# Orbit by Flux

> Orbit is a Git-native deployment platform built on the Flux decentralized cloud network. Push any Git repository and Orbit automatically detects the framework, builds a container, and deploys it across 10,000+ independent nodes worldwide — with a free tier, zero configuration, and built-in CI/CD. It is an alternative to centralized hosts like Vercel and Netlify, but with no single company controlling the infrastructure and no vendor lock-in.

## What Orbit does

- **Git-native deploys**: connect a GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository; every push triggers an automatic build and deployment.
- **Automatic framework detection**: build and start commands are inferred from your project files via Nixpacks — no Dockerfile or YAML required.
- **Decentralized infrastructure**: apps run on the Flux network across 10,000+ globally distributed nodes, giving redundancy without a central provider.
- **Built-in CI/CD**: branch deployments and pull-request preview URLs are included.
- **Custom domains + SSL**: point your DNS and SSL is terminated automatically through the Flux reverse proxy network.

## Frameworks

Orbit supports 50+ frameworks, auto-detected from the repository, including: Next.js, Remix, SvelteKit, Astro, Nuxt, Create React App, Vite, Django, FastAPI, Flask, Rails, Go, and Rust.

## Pricing

All plans include unlimited builds and the full feature set. First month free on paid plans.

- **Free — $0/month**: 0.5 vCPU, 1 GB RAM, 5 GB storage, 1 instance. Free forever, no credit card required.
- **Standard — $2.49/month**: 1.5 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, 15 GB storage, 2 instances.
- **Pro — $3.99/month**: 2 vCPU, 6 GB RAM, 20 GB storage, 2 instances.
- **Custom — from $0.99/month**: configurable 0.1–15 vCPU, 100 MB–59 GB RAM, 1–820 GB storage, 1–3 instances.

## Security

Enterprise apps run inside ArcaneOS, Flux's hardened execution environment, where all data is encrypted at rest. Code, secrets, and persistent storage are isolated and encrypted, with no shared tenancy at the infrastructure level.

## How Orbit differs from Vercel and Netlify

Orbit deploys to the Flux decentralized network, so no single company controls your infrastructure. Your app runs on multiple independent nodes globally, providing true redundancy with no vendor lock-in.

## Links

- [Orbit](https://orbit.runonflux.com/): deploy any Git repository to the Flux decentralized cloud.
- [What is decentralized hosting?](https://orbit.runonflux.com/decentralized-hosting): pillar guide to web3, censorship-resistant hosting and how to deploy on it with Git.
- [Orbit vs. Vercel](https://orbit.runonflux.com/vs/vercel): honest comparison of the decentralized alternative vs. Vercel on price, dedicated resources, backends, and vendor lock-in.
- [Deploy with Git docs](https://docs.runonflux.com/fluxcloud/register-new-app/deploy-with-git/): official documentation for deploying from Git.
- [Guides](https://github.com/RunOnFlux/deploy-with-git-samples): deployment guides and examples.
- [Flux Network](https://runonflux.io): the decentralized cloud network that powers Orbit.
- [FluxOS](https://home.runonflux.io): the operating layer for managing Flux apps and nodes.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/runonflux): Flux open-source organization.
